data.body:
PROGRESS — field validation probe fully de-staled + corrected + green on baseline AND HEAD.

Fixed (commit d8c1bda9):
1. CLI drift: 23 'rebar ticket <verb>' calls -> top-level 'rebar <verb>' across both probes (subagent confirmed zero other occurrences repo-wide).
2. macOS mktemp: trailing-XXXXXX fix.
3. Inbound assertions rewritten to the documented conflict_resolver FIELD_CLASSES policy: state (title/priority/status/assignee/type)=local-wins; additive (description/comments)=merge/local-first; set (labels)=union add-only. Phase 3a repurposed to a genuine inbound positive control (set-union label ADD lands on untouched ticket) + negative control (state title stays local-wins).
4. Phase 6a: reworked from false 1-pass-convergence to EVENTUAL idempotency (reconciler settles a Jira edit over 2-3 passes; still fails real non-convergence).
5. Added a clear FIELD SYNC MATRIX (per-field Class + Create/Update-out/Update-in + legend) documenting what is/isn't synced, with CRUD coverage.

Root cause of the original inbound failures was confirmed by a controlled live test: the reconciler pushes local->Jira and suppresses inbound for STATE fields (resolve_state returns local unconditionally) — i.e. the probe's expectations were wrong, not the code. Identical on baseline and HEAD (move-only split).

Live DIG result: 167 passed / 0 failed on the pre-split baseline (19f6dea5) AND on HEAD — identical parity. This is the full-pipeline confirmation that the acli split preserved Jira-sync behavior.

REMAINING: e2e_validation_probe.sh (lighter companion) got the de-stale fix but runs UNFILTERED bootstrap-strict; in a store with local-only tickets that is unsafe (would push them to DIG) + non-idempotent. It needs --filter-local-ids scoping (like the field probe) to be safe/green here — pending decision.
